summary_one_line: Input must carry a Feature record whose closure tag equals the 4-byte literal 'size' so the size-feature FeatureParams fixup branch is taken.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A FeatureList record whose tag bytes spell 'size' (0x73 0x69 0x7a 0x65) reaching OT::Feature::sanitize, so that the line-813 equality closure->tag == HB_TAG('s','i','z','e') holds and the true side of the line-812 compound gate is entered.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed (value_profile_cmplog, trial=2) contains the literal byte run 73 69 7a 65 ('size') at offset 0x39, satisfying the closure->tag == HB_TAG('s','i','z','e') equality, and the per-role overlay marks lines 815-826 (the size-feature fixup body) as winner-only [W].
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: None of the five loser-blocking seeds (cmplog) contain the byte run for 'size'; they carry GSUB/GPOS/GSHB table tags instead, so closure->tag never equals HB_TAG('s','i','z','e') and the line-812 gate stays false (L shows T=0 F=54 at line 812).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and value_profile_cmplog already have the input-to-state substitution mutator, so comparison-solving via substitution is NOT the differentiator. The winner adds value_profile: the CMP_MAP gradient buckets the Hamming/prefix distance of the 4-byte tag comparison at line 813, rewarding inputs that progressively match more bytes of 'size' and guiding the search to the exact literal that cmplog-only fails to assemble within budget.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker gate requires the closure tag to equal the 4-byte literal 'size'.
The blocked (true) side and its fixup body are exercised only by the winner.
The winner-resolving seed contains the literal byte run 'size' at offset 0x39.
In the loser arm the line-812 gate evaluates false 54 times and is never taken true on that row.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 812  T=1 F=0  T=0 F=54  if (featureParams == 0 && closure &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser-blocking seeds carry GSUB/GPOS table tags rather than the 'size' feature tag.
The decisive pair's differentiating technique is value_profile, with both fuzzers sharing cmplog's I2S stack.
value_profile_cmplog feedback includes CMP_MAP gradient buckets that cmplog-only lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient, not I2SRandReplace, so lineage dash-row verification is not mandatory. The decisive delta between value_profile_cmplog and cmplog is value_profile (both already have I2S), so the differentiator is the CMP_MAP gradient feedback over the line-813 4-byte tag equality, not I2S substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a 4-byte literal equality where value_profile_cmplog and cmplog reach the gate at statistically indistinguishable rates, showing the CMP_MAP gradient adds no advantage over I2S-only at this magic-tag width.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner-resolving seed is available, so the attribution of the value_profile advantage to CMP_MAP gradient over the 'size' tag comparison rests on a single positive byte example plus mechanism reasoning rather than multiple winner seeds.
